hooks.command: jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // .tool_response.filePath // empty' | { read -r f; case "$f" in *.swift) if command -v swiftformat >/dev/null; then swiftformat "$f" --quiet; fi ;; esac; } || true

CMD=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if printf '%s
' "$CMD" | grep -qE '(^|[;&|][[:space:]]*)gh pr create'; then printf '%s' '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PostToolUse","additionalContext":"A PR was just created. Project rule: run /codex-review (cross-vendor Codex review of this branch) now, triage its findings, and record the verdict in the PR before handing it over for merge."}}'; elif printf '%s
' "$CMD" | grep -qE '(^|[;&|][[:space:]]*)git push'; then printf '%s' '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PostToolUse","additionalContext":"A git push just ran. Before treating this work as done: check the CI result for the pushed commit (gh run watch --exit-status <run-id>, or gh pr checks <pr> --watch) and report the outcome. If CI is red, investigate and fix before moving on."}}'; elif printf '%s
' "$CMD" | grep -qE '(^|[;&|][[:space:]]*)git commit'; then printf '%s' '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PostToolUse","additionalContext":"A git commit just ran. Project rule: push this commit to the remote now (git push) and watch the CI result, so problems surface immediately instead of piling up."}}'; fi

CMD=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); case "$CMD" in *'git commit'*) case "$CMD" in *'[red]'*) exit 0 ;; esac; ENG="$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/Packages/EmbroideryEngine"; if [ -d "$ENG" ]; then OUT=$(cd "$ENG" && swift test 2>&1) || { echo 'Engine tests are red — commit blocked. Never commit failing engine tests (fix or finish the green phase first). A deliberate TDD red-phase commit is allowed with a [red] marker in the commit message.' >&2; echo "$OUT" | tail -30 >&2; exit 2; }; fi ;; esac
